decision.summary: Reporting-only split for generic Promise continuation call-return receivers. Generic call-return receivers now carry callee-identity-shaped missing evidence, but no new call-return receiver is admitted as PromiseLike producer proof.
policy: A call result used as `.then`/`.catch`/`.finally` receiver is not Promise proof by itself.
Missing callee identity remains an ambiguous-selector boundary.
Known direct/imported call targets still need returned PromiseLike domain proof before exact admission.
Dynamic/member call-return receivers remain closed until concrete callee identity and return-domain evidence are available.

corpus_source_scan.parser_revision: balanced call receiver extraction with same-file import and async-name census
limitations: Lexical source scan only; source prevalence prices follow-up work but does not admit semantics.
This scan uses a stronger balanced-call receiver extractor than the predecessor artifact, so occurrence counts are not a strict subset of the earlier 835 generic call-return rows.
Import and async-function candidates are same-file lexical candidates; they do not prove runtime identity.
Member call-return receivers can include ordinary objects, thenables, and Promise-producing APIs; exact admission must stay closed without callee identity plus returned PromiseLike domain evidence.

diagnostic_labels_added.label: promise-call-return-member-callee-proof
diagnostic_labels_added.exact_policy: closed until member receiver identity and returned PromiseLike domain proof exist

diagnostic_labels_added.label: promise-call-return-local-or-parameter-callee-proof
diagnostic_labels_added.exact_policy: closed until local/parameter callee identity and returned PromiseLike domain proof exist

diagnostic_labels_added.label: promise-call-return-imported-binding-callee-proof
diagnostic_labels_added.exact_policy: closed until import coordinate identity and returned PromiseLike domain proof exist

diagnostic_labels_added.label: promise-call-return-imported-member-callee-proof
diagnostic_labels_added.exact_policy: closed until imported namespace/member identity and returned PromiseLike domain proof exist

diagnostic_labels_added.label: promise-call-return-direct-function-return-domain-proof
diagnostic_labels_added.exact_policy: closed until a known direct function target has PromiseLike return-domain evidence

diagnostic_labels_added.label: promise-call-return-imported-function-return-domain-proof
diagnostic_labels_added.exact_policy: closed until a known imported function target has PromiseLike return-domain evidence

next_work: Do not open broad member call-return exact admission first; it is the largest bucket but also the most precision-sensitive.
Price a narrow same-file async-function return producer slice next: the lexical scan found 79 candidates across 10 repos, and the existing diagnostic label already maps it to scheduling proof.
After async-return proof, target local/imported call-return producer attribution only when the callee identity and returned PromiseLike domain are both explicit.
Keep constructor receivers and arbitrary thenables closed until executor timing, resolve/reject channels, and thenable assimilation hard negatives are in place.
